title: Git and Developer Tooling
summary: A practical tooling course covering filesystem and terminal fluency, Git's data model, commits, branches, remotes, collaboration, dependency/build hygiene, debugging, CI, secret safety and recovery.

assumed_knowledge: A current or planned code track
audience.target_capability: Independently create, inspect, share, review, build and recover a small version-controlled project without destructive guesswork or secret leakage.

includes: Git working tree, index, objects and refs
Issues, pull requests and code review
Dependency manifests, lockfiles and reproducible builds
Debugger, formatter, linter and static-analysis workflow
CI basics, secret hygiene, backup and safe recovery
non_goals: Shell scripting as a full language
Advanced history surgery on shared repositories
A specific CI vendor's complete feature set
Storing large binary datasets in ordinary Git

runtime.language: Git CLI with shell examples for PowerShell and a POSIX shell
runtime.execution_environment: Disposable local repositories and sandboxed remotes with no production credentials
notes: Destructive and history-rewriting demonstrations use disposable repositories only.
Commands are taught through state prediction, not rote recipes.

modules.title: Filesystem and terminal fluency
modules.description: Navigate and inspect project files safely across supported command environments.

skills.title: Paths and project trees
skills.description: Resolve absolute, relative, parent and home paths and account for spaces and case behavior.
outcomes: Navigate and explain a nested project tree without relying on the current GUI location.

skills.title: List, read and search
skills.description: List hidden files, inspect text and search names or content with bounded commands.
outcomes: Locate a configuration or symbol in a project without modifying files.

skills.title: Create, copy, move and rename
skills.description: Manipulate files and directories with quoted literal paths and verify destinations.
outcomes: Reorganize a disposable project tree and verify every resulting path.

skills.title: Command safety and help
skills.description: Read help, preview scope, avoid destructive wildcards and stop when a computed target is uncertain.
outcomes: Review a risky command sequence and propose a verifiable safer workflow.

modules.title: Repository model and first commit
modules.description: Understand Git state before changing it.

skills.title: Repository and working tree
skills.description: Distinguish project files, repository metadata, tracked state and untracked files.
outcomes: Initialize and inspect a repository without committing generated or secret files.

skills.title: Working tree, index and HEAD
skills.description: Predict differences among unstaged, staged and committed snapshots.
outcomes: Use status and diff variants to explain every changed path before committing.

skills.title: Ignore policy
skills.description: Ignore generated, machine-local and secret files without hiding required source or removing already tracked files by mistake.
outcomes: Create and test a project-appropriate ignore file.

skills.title: Selective staging and first commit
skills.description: Stage an intentional patch and create one coherent snapshot.
outcomes: Produce a commit whose staged diff matches its stated purpose.

modules.title: Commits and history
modules.description: Create navigable history and inspect how snapshots relate.

skills.title: Commit messages and scope
skills.description: Write concise imperative subjects and include rationale when it is not obvious from the diff.
outcomes: Split mixed changes and write messages that let a reviewer predict each commit.

skills.title: Log, show and graph
skills.description: Inspect commits, parents, authorship, patches and branch topology.
outcomes: Locate and explain the commit that introduced a supplied change.

skills.title: Diffs and patch review
skills.description: Read line-level changes in working, staged and historical comparisons.
outcomes: Review a patch for unintended files, whitespace-only churn and behavior changes.

skills.title: Tags and release points
skills.description: Use annotated tags to name reviewed immutable release commits.
outcomes: Create, inspect and verify a version tag on the intended commit.

modules.title: Branches, merges and conflicts
modules.description: Develop isolated changes and integrate them with deliberate history.

skills.title: Branches as movable refs
skills.description: Relate branch names, HEAD and commits without treating branches as copied folders.
outcomes: Draw and manipulate a small branch graph while predicting HEAD movement.

skills.title: Short-lived feature branches
skills.description: Create, switch, update and delete branches around one coherent change.
outcomes: Complete an isolated feature branch and verify the base and tip before integration.

skills.title: Fast-forward and three-way merge
skills.description: Predict when Git moves a ref or creates a merge commit and choose a repository policy deliberately.
outcomes: Perform and explain fast-forward and divergent merges in a disposable repository.

skills.title: Conflict resolution
skills.description: Interpret base, ours and theirs, edit the intended combined result and test before completing integration.
outcomes: Resolve a content conflict and document why the final behavior is correct.

modules.title: Remotes and synchronization
modules.description: Exchange commits without confusing remote-tracking refs and local work.

skills.title: Clone and remote configuration
skills.description: Create a local repository from a remote and inspect names, URLs and default branches.
outcomes: Clone a sandbox repository and explain each local and remote-tracking ref.

skills.title: Fetch and compare
skills.description: Update remote-tracking refs without altering the working branch and inspect divergence first.
outcomes: Fetch and explain ahead, behind and diverged states before integration.

skills.title: Pull policy
skills.description: Understand pull as fetch plus configured integration and choose merge, rebase or fast-forward-only policy intentionally.
outcomes: Predict and perform a pull under an explicit policy in a disposable repository.

skills.title: Push and non-fast-forward rejection
skills.description: Publish local refs only after reviewing destination, upstream and remote divergence.
outcomes: Push a feature branch and safely resolve a rejected update without force-overwriting others.

modules.title: Issues, pull requests and review
modules.description: Coordinate changes through explicit requirements and reviewable evidence.

skills.title: Actionable issues
skills.description: Describe problem, context, reproduction or desired outcome and acceptance criteria.
outcomes: Write a scoped issue that another learner can understand and verify.

skills.title: Pull request narrative
skills.description: Explain what changed, why, validation evidence, risks and follow-up without restating every diff line.
outcomes: Open a focused pull request linked to acceptance criteria and tests.

skills.title: Code review
skills.description: Review correctness, tests, security, readability and scope with specific respectful comments.
outcomes: Submit prioritized review feedback and distinguish blocker, suggestion and question.

skills.title: Review updates and integration
skills.description: Respond to review, keep evidence current and merge only an approved passing revision.
outcomes: Revise, re-review and integrate a pull request while preserving traceability.

modules.title: Dependencies and reproducible builds
modules.description: Make local and automated environments produce the same declared result.

skills.title: Dependency manifests and lockfiles
skills.description: Declare direct dependencies, review lockfile changes and avoid committing installed dependency directories.
outcomes: Install and reproduce a project from reviewed manifest and lockfile data.

skills.title: Build and task commands
skills.description: Expose documented, non-interactive build, test, lint and run commands through the language's normal tooling.
outcomes: Run a clean checkout through setup, build, test and start without undocumented manual steps.

skills.title: Configuration and environment
skills.description: Separate defaults, environment-specific configuration and secrets and document required variables safely.
outcomes: Configure a project from an example environment file without committing credentials.

skills.title: Generated artifacts and releases
skills.description: Distinguish reproducible outputs from source and attach verified artifacts to versioned releases rather than ordinary commits.
outcomes: Define and test an artifact policy for a small project.

modules.title: Debugging and quality automation
modules.description: Use repeatable tools and history evidence to isolate defects.

skills.title: Debugger workflow
skills.description: Set breakpoints, step, inspect variables and test a hypothesis rather than editing blindly.
outcomes: Record and explain a debugger-assisted root-cause investigation.

skills.title: Formatter and linter
skills.description: Automate style and static checks through one documented command with minimal unrelated churn.
outcomes: Configure and apply formatter and linter checks to changed files.

skills.title: History-based regression isolation
skills.description: Use a reproducible good/bad test and binary search over commits to find a regression introduction.
outcomes: Locate a seeded regression with bisect and verify the causative patch.

skills.title: Local hooks and server checks
skills.description: Use fast local feedback as convenience while keeping authoritative checks reproducible outside developer hooks.
outcomes: Design a check placement plan for local, pre-push and CI stages.

modules.title: CI, security and recovery
modules.description: Automate verification, protect credentials and recover from common mistakes with auditable actions.

skills.title: Continuous integration pipeline
skills.description: Run clean setup, build, lint and tests on proposed changes with least required permissions.
outcomes: Create and explain a minimal CI pipeline whose result gates integration.

skills.title: Secret prevention and response
skills.description: Keep secrets out of repositories, logs and artifacts and rotate exposed values rather than merely deleting a commit.
outcomes: Review a leak scenario and execute a safe containment, rotation and history-remediation plan in a sandbox.

skills.title: Restore, revert, reset and reflog
skills.description: Choose working-tree restoration, a new inverse commit, local ref movement or reflog recovery based on publication state.
outcomes: Recover staged, committed and lost-ref mistakes in disposable repositories without overwriting shared history.

skills.title: Collaborative repository capstone
skills.description: Integrate issue, branch, commits, review, build, tests, CI, security and release evidence.
outcomes: Deliver and explain a tagged collaborative change from scoped issue through passing reviewed integration.

exit_outcomes: Predict and inspect working-tree, index, commit, branch and remote states before changing them.
Create focused history, resolve conflicts and collaborate through scoped issues, pull requests and reviews.
Reproduce dependency setup, builds, tests, formatting and analysis from a clean checkout and CI job.
Prevent secret leakage and recover safely from common working-tree, index, commit and ref mistakes.
Deliver a tagged reviewed change with traceable requirements and verification evidence.
